back_translation: And David went from there and escaped to the cave of Adullam, and his brothers and all the house of his father heard, and they went down to him there.
And there gathered to him every man of distress, and every man who had a creditor, and every man of bitter soul, and he became over them as leader. And they were with him about four hundred men.
And David went from there to Mizpeh of Moab, and said to the king of Moab, 'Let my father and my mother please come out and stay with you, until I know what God will do for me.'
And he committed them to the face of the king of Moab, and they stayed with him all the days that David was in the stronghold.
And Gad the prophet said to David, 'Do not stay in the stronghold; go and come into the land of Judah.' So David went and came to the forest of Hereth.

And Saul heard that David was discovered, and the men who were with him; and Saul was sitting at Gibeah, under the tamarisk tree at Ramah, with his spear in his hand, and all his servants standing around him.

And Saul said to his servants who stood around him, 'Hear now, sons of Benjamin — will the son of Jesse give to all of you fields and vineyards, will he make all of you commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds?

That all of you have conspired against me, and there is no one who uncovers my ear when my son made a covenant with the son of Jesse, and there is no one of you who is grieved for me and uncovers my ear, that my son has set up my servant against me to lie in wait, as on this day?'
And Doeg the Edomite answered, and he stood over the servants of Saul, and said, 'I saw the son of Jesse come to Nob, to Ahimelech son of Ahitub.
And he inquired of the LORD for him, and provisions he gave him, and the sword of Goliath the Philistine he gave him.'
And the king sent to call Ahimelech son of Ahitub the priest, and all the house of his father, the priests who were at Nob. And all of them came to the king.
And Saul said, 'Hear now, son of Ahitub.' And he said, 'Here I am, my lord.'

And Saul said to him, 'Why have you conspired against me, you and the son of Jesse, in giving him bread and a sword, and inquiring for him of God, to rise up against me to lie in wait, as on this day?'
And Ahimelech answered the king and said, 'And who among all your servants is like David — faithful, the king's son-in-law, and turned to your bodyguard, and honored in your house?

This day was the first time I inquired for him of God? Far be it from me! Let not the king place upon his servant any matter against all the house of my father, for your servant has known nothing of all this, neither small nor great.'
And the king said, 'You shall surely die, Ahimelech — you and all the house of your father.'

And the king said to the guards who stood over him, 'Turn and kill the priests of the LORD, because their hand also is with David, and because they knew that he was fleeing, and they did not uncover my ear.' But the servants of the king were not willing to send out their hand to strike the priests of the LORD.
And the king said to Doeg, 'You turn and strike at the priests.' And Doeg the Edomite turned and struck the priests himself, and put to death on that day eighty-five men who wore the linen ephod.
And Nob the city of the priests he struck with the edge of the sword, from man to woman, from child to nursing infant, and ox, donkey, and sheep with the edge of the sword.
And one son of Ahimelech son of Ahitub escaped, and his name was Abiathar, and he fled after David.
And Abiathar told David that Saul had killed the priests of the LORD.
And David said to Abiathar, 'I knew in that day, when Doeg the Edomite was there, that he would surely tell Saul. I am responsible for every soul of the house of your father.
Stay with me, do not fear; for he who seeks my soul seeks your soul; for safekeeping you have with me.'
